title: Update CI to fail on Podman 4.0 errors
body:
CI has been testing against containers/podman/main to report issues.
With the release of Podman 4.0, any errors are now failures.

* Makefile target "test" and "lint" updated to use tox covering
  python 3.6, 3.8, 3.9 and 3.10.
* Added black formatting check to Makefile target "lint"
* Source code changes made to satisfy "tox -e black-format" and
  "tox -e pylint"

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: Okay, so it appears like the new image "works" (getting actual test errors now).  That means the dedicated image I'd like to build, will most likely work well here.  Thanks for trying that.

@jwhonce I've got the new dedicated image coming in via https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/161  Assuming that passes (and no further package changes are needed), you'll want to merge/rebase on that to use the new image here.
I merged, you need to rebase.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
